scenario_description: The user first asks to sort by Promotion Type, then changes to sorting by Revenue descending.

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Help me sort by the Promotion Type column
Don't sort by that column. Sort by the Revenue column from largest to smallest instead. Undo the previous sort first, then save

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/EntireSummerSales.xlsx'); rev_cols = [c for c in df.columns if 'revenue' in str(c).lower()]; ok = len(rev_cols) > 0 and df[rev_cols[0]].is_monotonic_decreasing; print('PASSED' if ok else 'FAILED')"
